comment: Flow_BoxFolderCreate (Phase 7 / ADR-0012 / build-plan 04 §5, Wave 1 / box-flow-patterns Fragment 1). Returnable CHILD (Request trigger + Response) that mints the ONE UPPERCASE Case/PO Box folder at parse-confirm. Invoked from CS Intake INSIDE Scope_generate_casepo AFTER Update_case_casepo (where cr1bd_casepo first exists) with { caseId, casePo, workProviderId } — that intake invocation is an operator/business-phase LIVE edit (the repo intake.definition.json TRAILS live; do NOT add the call to the stale repo def — see docs/plans/phase-7-box-integration/box-integration-activation.md and memory intake-repo-trails-live). HOUSE RULES: the gate cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_AT_INTAKE_ENABLED is READ not defined (dataverse owns the name+default OFF); the folder is created via the CUSTOM connector shared_box_rest CreateFolder (op fronts the box-webhook Azure Function which mints the Box CCG token server-side — custom connectors cannot run client_credentials, Microsoft Learn); BYTES never touch this connector (finalize keeps first-party shared_box CreateFile). IDEMPOTENT two ways: (1) in-flow guard empty(cr1bd_boxfolderid) skips the call when already stamped; (2) the Function swallows Box 409 item_name_in_use (case-insensitive) and returns the existing folder id. parent.id comes from env-var cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_ROOT_ID via the BoxArchiveRootId param (NEVER a hardcoded live Box id). DATAVERSE DEPENDENCY: stamps cr1bd_boxfolderid (DURABLE, idempotency-critical) then cr1bd_boxsyncedat (BEST-EFFORT) in TWO DECOUPLED UpdateRecords — the durable id stamp + outcome=created must NOT be gated on the timestamp write, so a minted folder is always recorded as 'created' even if cr1bd_boxsyncedat is rejected (otherwise the folder is orphaned: id unstamped, outcome stuck at init 'gated_off', idempotency guard permanently re-colliding). cr1bd_boxsyncedat (DateTime, UserLocal, required:none) is the build-plan-04 §step-2 flow contract column and is read by box-blob-purge; the dataverse agent's shipped case.json declares cr1bd_boxfolderid but NOT YET cr1bd_boxsyncedat — that column must be added by dataverse-data-architect before activation (same close-the-drift pattern as cr1bd_finalizedpayloadhash); until it exists, the decoupled best-effort timestamp write simply fails harmlessly. [BUILD] state=off. ACTIVATION [RESERVED-FOR-USER]: import the cr1bd_box_rest connector, bind the connection (Function host key), set cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_ROOT_ID, flip BOX_API_ENABLED then BOX_FOLDER_AT_INTAKE_ENABLED in a test env. Claude never holds a Box credential, never binds the connection, never flips the gate.

metadata.description: Parent Box folder id for archives, sourced from env-var cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_ROOT_ID at activation. Flow parameter; NEVER a hardcoded live Box id.

manual.comment: Invoked by CS Intake (Run-a-Child-Flow) inside Scope_generate_casepo after Update_case_casepo, and by case-resolve's survivor-folder ensure. Single input { caseId, casePo, workProviderId }. workProviderId is carried for caller symmetry/observability only and is NOT load-bearing inside the child (the folder name is derived from casePo); a merged-case caller may pass '' safely.

Init_outcome.comment: One of: created | exists | gated_off | skipped_no_casepo. Returned to the caller for observability.

Init_boxFolderId.comment: The Box folder id (new from CreateFolder, or the pre-existing one read off the case). Declared top-level (InitializeVariable cannot be nested).

Get_gate_definition.comment: READ the gate (never DEFINE it). Same idiom as enrich/finalize: coalesce currentValue -> defaultValue -> 'false'.

Set_gate_BOX_FOLDER_AT_INTAKE_ENABLED.comment: Default OFF -> the gate-off branch responds outcome=gated_off and never calls Box.

If_gate_on.comment: Gate-on path only. Gate-off leaves outcome=gated_off (set at init).

Get_case_for_box.comment: Re-read the case for the idempotency guard: cr1bd_boxfolderid (already stamped?) + the authoritative cr1bd_casepo.

If_already_stamped.comment: IDEMPOTENCY + safety guard. If cr1bd_boxfolderid is already set -> no-op (outcome=exists). If there is no Case/PO at all (neither on the case nor passed in) -> skip (outcome=skipped_no_casepo): never CreateFolder with an empty name. Else fall through to create.

Create_box_folder.comment: CUSTOM connector CreateFolder -> POST /2.0/folders { name: UPPERCASE Case/PO, parent.id: archive root }. The Function swallows Box 409 item_name_in_use (case-insensitive collision) and returns the existing folder id, so a concurrent/re-run create is harmless. body/parent/id is the ONLY id field and it is an @parameter (env-var), never a literal — the folder NAME is the UPPERCASE Case/PO (not all-digits). BYTES never go through this connector.

Stamp_boxfolderid.comment: DURABLE stamp of the live Box folder id ONLY — the idempotency-critical write (the guard empty(cr1bd_boxfolderid) depends on it). Deliberately DECOUPLED from the cr1bd_boxsyncedat timestamp write (Stamp_boxsyncedat below): a successful CreateFolder must persist the id and report outcome=created even if the not-yet-guaranteed cr1bd_boxsyncedat column write fails (dataverse-data-architect must declare cr1bd_boxsyncedat on cr1bd_case before activation — it ships cr1bd_boxfolderid but not yet this one). Without the split, a rejected timestamp would fail the whole UpdateRecord, leaving the minted folder unrecorded, outcome stuck at its init 'gated_off', and the idempotency guard permanently re-colliding.

Set_outcome_created.comment: Report created off the DURABLE id stamp (Stamp_boxfolderid), NOT the best-effort timestamp — a folder that was minted and whose id persisted is genuinely 'created' even if the timestamp write later fails.

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: @concat('Box folder ', variables('boxFolderId'), ' (', toUpper(coalesce(outputs('Get_case_for_box')?['body/cr1bd_casepo'], variables('casePo'))), ') minted under archive root')

Audit_box_folder_created.comment: Audit the create (action box_folder_created=100000019, Info). Linked to the case. runAfter the DURABLE id stamp so the audit only records a genuine, persisted create.

Stamp_boxsyncedat.comment: BEST-EFFORT stamp of the sync timestamp ONLY. cr1bd_boxsyncedat is the build-plan-04 flow-contract column (DateTime, UserLocal) read by box-blob-purge; dataverse-data-architect must declare it on cr1bd_case before activation (close-the-drift like cr1bd_finalizedpayloadhash). DECOUPLED from the id stamp + outcome on purpose: this runs LAST and is allowed to fail without losing the durable boxfolderid or corrupting outcome=created — so a missing/rejected column degrades to 'no timestamp yet' instead of orphaning the folder. runAfter the audit on ALL completion outcomes so a sub-step failure cannot suppress this write either.

Respond_result.comment: Child-flow return (required to be callable via Run-a-Child-Flow). { caseId, boxFolderId, outcome (created|exists|gated_off|skipped_no_casepo), folderPath }. folderPath is built from the SAME coalesce(Get_case_for_box.cr1bd_casepo, casePo) the create/audit/guard steps use (NOT the trigger-passed casePo alone) so a merged-case survivor caller passing casePo='' still returns the real UPPERCASE folder path; on the gate-off / skipped_no_casepo branches Get_case_for_box did not run (its ?[] output is null) and casePo is empty, so the coalesce is '' and folderPath honestly returns '' rather than a misleading bare '/'. runAfter If_gate_on on ALL completion outcomes so the child always returns even if a sub-step failed.
